params.name: before
params.description:
Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Returns only transactions created before the specified date.
Provides an explicit end time. If not provided, default value will be applied, and may change over time. 
The current default value is the past 90 days.

params.name: after
params.description:
Unix timestamp in milliseconds. Returns only transactions created after the specified date.
Provides an explicit start time. If not provided, default value will be applied, and may change over time. 
The current default value is the past 90 days.

params.name: status
params.description: You can filter by one of the statuses.

params.name: orderBy
params.description:
The field to order the results by.

**Note:** Ordering by a field that is not `createdAt` may result in transactions that receive updates as you request the next or previous pages of results, resulting in missing those transactions.

params.name: sort
params.description: The direction to order the results by

params.name: limit
params.description: Limits the number of results. If not provided, a limit of 200 will be used. The maximum allowed limit is 500

params.name: sourceType
params.description: The source type of the transaction

params.name: sourceId
params.description: The source ID of the transaction

params.name: destType
params.description: The destination type of the transaction

params.name: destId
params.description: The destination ID of the transaction

params.name: assets
params.description: A list of assets to filter by, seperated by commas

params.name: txHash
params.description: Returns only results with a specified txHash

params.name: sourceWalletId
params.description: Returns only results where the source is a specific end user wallet

params.name: destWalletId
params.description: Returns only results where the destination is a specific end user wallet

items.docs:
Get the transaction history for your workspace.

**Endpoint Permissions:** Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor, Viewer.

Creates a new transaction. This endpoint can be used for regular Transfers, Contract Calls, Raw & Typed message signing.
- For Transfers, the required parameters are: `assetId`, `source`, `destination` and `amount`.

- For Contract Calls, the required parameters are: `operation.CONTRACT_CALL`, `assetId` (Base Asset), `source`, `destination`, `amount` (usually 0) and `extraParameters` object with `contractCallData` string.
- For Solana Program Calls, the required parameters are: `operation.PROGRA

Estimates the transaction fee for a specific transaction request.
This endpoint simulates a transaction which means that the system will expect to have the requested asset and balance in the specified wallet.


**Note**: Supports all Fireblocks assets except ZCash (ZEC).
Learn more about Fireblocks Fee Management in the following [guide](https://developers.fireblocks.com/reference/estimate-transaction-fee).
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor.

info.name: Get a specific transaction by Fireblocks transaction ID

params.name: txId
params.description: The ID of the transaction to return

items.docs:
Get a specific transaction data by Fireblocks Transaction ID
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor, Viewer.

info.name: Get a specific transaction by external transaction ID

params.name: externalTxId
params.description: The external ID of the transaction to return

items.docs:
Returns transaction by external transaction ID.
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor, Viewer.

info.name: Set confirmation threshold by Fireblocks Transaction ID

params.name: txId
params.description: The ID of the transaction

items.docs:
Overrides the required number of confirmations for transaction completion Fireblocks Transaction ID.
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor.

params.name: txId
params.description: The ID of the transaction

items.docs:
Drops a stuck ETH (EVM) transaction and creates a replacement transaction with 0 amount.
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor.

params.name: txId
params.description: The Fireblocks Transaction ID of the transaction to cancel

items.docs:
Cancels a transaction by Fireblocks Transaction ID.

Can be used only for transactions that did not get to the BROADCASTING state.
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor.

params.name: txId
params.description: The ID of the transaction to freeze

items.docs:
Freezes a transaction by ID.

Usually used for AML integrations when the incoming funds should be quarantined.
For account based assets - the entire amount of the transaction is frozen 
For UTXO based assets - all UTXOs of the specified transaction are frozen
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin.

params.name: txId
params.description: The ID of the transaction to unfreeze

items.docs:
Unfreezes a transaction by Fireblocks Transaction ID and makes the transaction available again.
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin.

info.name: Set confirmation threshold by transaction hash

params.name: txHash
params.description: The TxHash

items.docs:
Overrides the required number of confirmations for transaction completion by transaction hash.
</br>Endpoint Permission: Admin, Non-Signing Admin, Signer, Approver, Editor.

info.name: Get an approval request by id

items.docs: Get an approval request by id

info.name: Cancel an approval request by ID

items.docs:
Cancel an approval request by id.
- Can only cancel requests in the `PENDING` status.
- Returns `202 Accepted` when the cancellation is processed.

params.description: The requested job id

items.docs: Stop the given job immediately. If the job is in the ‘Active’ state, the job will be canceled after completing the current task. Vault accounts and Wallets that are already created will not be affected.
